text.heading: Kano Power Project Doomed
text.p: Alhaji Mohammadu Danpass has accused the ruling All Nigeria Peoples Party (ANPP) government in Kano State of improverishing the people, stating that the situation of electricity in Kano has never been as bad as currently obtained.

Danpass, who was campaign director to Dr. Abubakar Rimi during the 2003 presidential election, added that the proposed Independent Power Project (IPP) would not see the light of day till the end of tenure of the present dispensation.
According to him, the IPP projects is still mere paperwork and would suffer the same fate as the N5.6b water project is as the government has never been sincere with the people of Kano.

Because of the government's insensitivity towards the plight of the people, business activities have been on a downhill slope and signing a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with two companies to build IPP plants in two years is not what the people want to hear according to Danpass.

Danpass went further to state that Kano is no longer a commercial center in the country but a corner shop State due to the situation where people now open shops instead of establishing indigenous industries.
He made an example of the textile industry which according to him as seen a 50% drop in patronage in the last two years after being one of the busiest market sectors in the country.

The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) chieftain therefore called on the leadership of the ANPP in the State to put politics aside and face the reality on ground, adding the situation could go out of hand if left unchecked.
